item.title: Ohio’s drag on free speech
item.description: The state’s restrictions are clearly an attempt to chill protected expression.

item.title: Who knows you best?
item.description: Edith Pritchett cartoon on how well algorithms know people.

item.title: Yet another step toward state capitalism
item.description: Competing with China by imitating its methods won’t work.

item.title: Another government shutdown that won’t end
item.description: Politicians talk a big game about reform but have accomplished nothing by withholding funding.

item.title: A free-speech farce in Finland
item.description: It’s not a crime to publish unpopular religious views.

item.title: Ask this question on a first date — or brace for a nasty surprise
item.description: Hot topics, AI dream girls and the Kennedy Center, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: We run small businesses. This is what Trump’s tariffs did to us.
item.description: Layoffs, refunds, Graham Platner and the gold Trump coin, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: Opening the Strait of Hormuz is the easy part
item.description: A brief history of sea mines, the weapon likely standing in President Donald Trump’s way.

item.title: Cartoon by Rob Rogers
item.description: Rob Rogers cartoon on verdict in Meta, YouTube, social media addiction trial.

item.title: What happened at LaGuardia Airport?
item.description: Making sense of this week’s crash — and how to reform air-traffic control.

item.title: In Israel, wartime reality doesn’t match what you see on the internet
item.description: Both the liberal internationalist left and the isolationist right are being duped by online propaganda.

item.title: Local control in jeopardy if Virginia mandates collective bargaining
item.description: Spanberger can show seriousness on affordability by vetoing a bill that would lead to higher property taxes.

item.title: Trump’s bluff in Iran is a ‘disaster’ of his own
item.description: Iran is exposing the limits of a presidency built on bluff, improvisation and submission rituals.

item.title: The unreligious religiosity of Christian identity politics
item.description: The new religious right is distinct from the one that was once led by evangelicals.

item.title: What gladiatorial politics will bury in the midterms
item.description: Never underestimate the Democrats’ ability to make a sow’s ear out of a silk purse.

item.title: In the age of AI, your digital identity needs this protection
item.description: Every individual should have power to control their own digital identity.

item.title: A tug of war over stablecoins could tear the U.S. dollar
item.description: If U.S. banks win this fight, China does too.

item.title: Win the Middle East, lose the Pacific?
item.description: Anti-China allies are feeling economic pain that Americans aren’t.

item.title: A blow against free speech in California
item.description: In defense of personal and parental responsibility on social media.

item.title: Trump isn’t hiding his election power grab. He’s bragging about it.
item.description: The administration is pursuing multiple paths to gaining control over November’s vote.

item.title: Any way the wind blows
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Trump, Iran war, peace talks.

item.title: China, the U.S. and the great electric vehicle race
item.description: A new super-fast charging battery from BYD is a wake-up call to American firms.

item.title: Want to live longer? Do this important exercise.
item.description: Aerobic exercise is important for health, but don’t forget strength training

item.title: Meta, YouTube found liable in addiction trial
item.description:
An upcoming Washington Post Opinions show. Stay tuned.
The Washington Post is owned by Jeff Bezos, who founded Amazon and Blue Origin.

item.title: Why does America need a surgeon general, anyway?
item.description: A simple solution to Casey Means’s stalled nomination: eliminate the position.

item.title: No, Trump is not losing his nerve on Iran
item.description: Three weeks that could change the Middle East forever.

item.title: Robert Mueller was a true American hero. He should be eulogized as such.
item.description: Robert S. Mueller III and DHS funding, in readers eyes

item.title: A new nightmare awaits Americans at the airport
item.description: Immigration agents with little public trust and training add to stress of flying for Americans.
